title: 4x4 X-Base Pedestal Dining Table
summary: A dining table on a single chunky 4x4 X-pedestal built around a center hub post — solid 4x4 stock throughout, unlike this catalog's thinner built-up 2x4 X-legs — topped with a glued-up planked top.
description:
This pedestal is built from solid 4x4 stock around a central hub post, not from thinner 2x4s crossed and joined at their intersection the way this catalog's other X-base table does — that's a genuinely different, chunkier pedestal architecture, not just a size variant. Eight 4x4 pieces, each cut with matching 45° angles, radiate out from the hub to form the visible X splay, anchored by one long diagonal 4x4 stretcher cut at 30° and two shorter 30°-angled legs.

**Every angled cut here is a longest-point measurement** — mark and cut these carefully, and dry-fit the whole pedestal before any glue goes on, since eight angled pieces meeting one hub compounds any small error fast. Small 4x4 and 1x4 blocking pieces, plus two 2x4 pieces, close out the pedestal assembly and give the apron above something solid to land on.

The 2x4 apron frames the top of the pedestal, and the tabletop is 1x4 boards glued up edge to edge and **cut to fit your own finished apron size** — the source design doesn't give a fixed plank count, since table width varies by preference.

tools.note: For the pedestal dry-fit and the tabletop glue-up.

materials.name: Primer and paint, or stain and poly

cutList.note: Longest point measurement, both ends cut at 45° off square, not parallel.
Longest point measurement, both ends cut at 30° off square, not parallel.
Longest point measurement, one end cut at 30° off square.
One end cut at 30° off square, longest point measurement.
One end cut at 30° off square, longest point measurement.
Both ends cut at 30° off square, longest point measurement, ends not parallel.
Glued up edge to edge and cut to fit your own finished apron size.

steps.title: Dry-fit the pedestal
steps.body: Cut the hub post, eight X splay legs, diagonal stretcher, and short legs, then dry-fit the whole pedestal before gluing — errors compound fast across eight angled pieces meeting one hub.

steps.title: Glue and fasten the pedestal
steps.body: Glue and fasten the pedestal assembly with lag screws, then add the small blocking and 2x4 pieces to close it out.

steps.title: Build the apron
steps.body: Assemble the 1x4 apron frame on top of the pedestal.

steps.title: Build and attach the top
steps.body: Glue up the 2x6 boards edge to edge, cut to fit your finished apron size, then attach to the apron.

steps.title: Finish
steps.body: Sand all surfaces and apply primer and paint, or stain and poly.
materials: Primer and paint, or stain and poly

unresolvedImages.alt: 4x4 X-base pedestal dining table with planked top
